title: The Most Awesome Movie of 2022, and 10 More to Stream Right Now. Plus, David Cronenberg!
format: Sean flies solo through ten movies worth streaming this week, from an Indian action epic to a Cronenberg-adjacent clone thriller, then sits down with David Cronenberg to talk Crimes of the Future, aging, and giving actors no direction at all.

films.title: RRR
films.note: S.S. Rajamouli's Telugu epic, 185 minutes of historical fantasy, musical numbers, and buddy comedy about two Indian revolutionaries fighting the British Raj. On Netflix, and briefly the highest-grossing Indian film ever before KGF: Chapter 2 overtook it.

films.title: Chip 'n Dale: Rescue Rangers
films.note: Akiva Schaffer's live-action/animation mashup, with Andy Samberg and John Mulaney voicing the title chipmunks thirty years after their show got canceled. On Disney+; Sean calls it the spiritual cousin of Who Framed Roger Rabbit, minus the same execution.

films.title: Fire Island
films.note: Andrew Ahn's Pride and Prejudice riff, written by and starring Joel Kim Booster alongside Bowen Yang, set among friends vacationing on Fire Island. On Hulu; originally developed for Quibi.

films.title: The Sadness
films.note: Rob Jabbaz's Taiwanese outbreak movie, a couple reuniting as a pandemic turns people homicidal. Extremely graphic splatter horror, on Shudder.

films.title: Jackass 4.5
films.note: The traditional making-of/deleted-scenes companion to Jackass Forever, with Jeff Tremaine, Spike Jonze, and Johnny Knoxville narrating the stunts that didn't make the cut. On Netflix, oddly, while Jackass Forever itself is on Paramount+.

films.title: Navalny
films.note: Daniel Roher's documentary following Alexei Navalny's investigation into his own poisoning, shot like a spy thriller. On HBO Max after CNN+ folded before it could premiere there.

films.title: Dual
films.note: Riley Stearns' deadpan sci-fi: in a world where the terminally ill can order clones of themselves, Karen Gillan has to fight hers to the death, trained by Aaron Paul. On AMC+.

films.title: Nitram
films.note: Justin Kurzel's drama on the life of the man behind Australia's 1996 Port Arthur massacre, with Caleb Landry Jones, Judy Davis, Essie Davis, and Anthony LaPaglia. Deliberately hard to watch, and, Sean says, non-judgmental about it. On AMC+.

films.title: Apollo 10½: A Space Age Childhood
films.note: Richard Linklater's rotoscoped memory piece, narrated by Jack Black, about growing up in Houston during the moon landing. On Netflix.

films.title: Tony Hawk: Until the Wheels Fall Off
films.note: Sam Jones' documentary on Tony Hawk still attempting tricks in his fifties that he couldn't land at 28. On HBO Max.

films.title: Crimes of the Future
films.note: Cronenberg's return to body horror after eight years away, from a script he wrote two decades ago and shelved. Stars Viggo Mortensen, Léa Seydoux, Kristen Stewart, and Scott Speedman; produced by Robert Lantos, who convinced him it had only gotten more relevant.

films.title: eXistenZ
films.note: Cited for its all-CG twin-headed lizard creature, a sign of how far the visual-effects toolkit has come since.

films.title: Spider
films.note: Ralph Fiennes told Cronenberg it was the least direction he'd ever gotten from a filmmaker, and meant it as a compliment.

films.title: Possessor
films.note: His son Brandon's film, shot in Budapest the same year Cronenberg shot Crimes of the Future in Athens.

films.title: Dead Ringers
films.note: Rachel Weisz is producing a new series adaptation; Cronenberg passed on getting involved, feeling he'd already said what he had to say with it.

films.title: Eastern Promises
films.note: Vincent Cassel's performance here is why Cronenberg wants him for his next film, The Shrouds.

films.title: Cosmopolis
films.note: The film Cronenberg once assumed would be his last.

films.title: Maps to the Stars
films.note: His actual most recent film before Crimes of the Future, which came together after a decade of working with Bruce Wagner.

films.title: Scanners
films.note: Almost became a network series at CBS, NBC, and ABC in the past, until the networks wanted the exploding heads taken out.

films: I Am Trying to Break Your Heart: A Film About Wilco
